title: × aegilotriticum sancti-andreae
intro: × Aegilotriticum sancti-andreae, also known as the Saint Andrew's cross, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the wheat family and is often found in fields and meadows. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of × Aegilotriticum sancti-andreae include its potential to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and fever, as well as its ability to support the immune system. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
